decision_summary: Traceability cleanup maps and PDF source_text machine-match waiver are accepted for F0 evaluation preparation with explicit limitations. This does not mutate raw artifacts, effective assessments, or the accepted 143-item final assessment set. E4 persistence and Streamlit workspace remain pending before the full P0 workflow can be treated as closed.
basis: 143 accepted current assessment set has passed evidence contract and AnalysisRun validation.
final_advisor_result_corrected.json has passed Advisor validation with no forbidden phrase or schema warnings.
Traceability findings affect audit precision and thesis wording boundaries, not a known systematic change to five-category verdicts.
Requirement-level metrics can proceed if they use the cleanup map rather than raw requirement IDs.
Evidence binding can support review if cleanup map is used, while E4 persistence should normalize supporting_evidence_ids to evidence_id.
PDF source_text machine matching is waived because PyPDF2 full-string matching is unreliable for table layout and line-break ordering.
accepted: 143 accepted current assessment set can enter F0 evaluation preparation
final_advisor_result_corrected.json can enter Advisor usefulness review
traceability cleanup maps can be used as effective metric mapping inputs

waived_with_reason.reason: Dot-level requirement IDs, parent/intro compilation nodes, and a small number of parent disclosure references are accepted only when requirement-level statistics use requirement_id_cleanup_map.json as the effective mapping layer.

Mixed supporting_evidence_ids references to chunk_id and evidence_id are sufficient for F-stage review when evidence_binding_cleanup_map.json uniquely maps them, but E4 persistence and Streamlit display should normalize bindings to evidence_id.

PyPDF2 matched only 28 of 159 substantive evidence snippets because of PDF table layout and text order limitations. F-stage and thesis wording may state page-level traceability and manual waiver, but must not claim every source_text snippet was machine-verified against the PDF.

effective_metric_policy_for_f.requirement_level_metrics: Use requirement_id_cleanup_map.json for E3 ordinary current-gap requirement references; use E3.5 reviewed artifacts as accepted unless a separate E3.5 traceability issue is identified.
effective_metric_policy_for_f.evidence_location_metrics: Use source_page, report_page_label, evidence_kind, chunk_id, smoke review results, and pdf_source_text_location_waiver.json limitations; do not report full machine-verbatim match coverage.
effective_metric_policy_for_f.advisor_usefulness_review: Use final_advisor_result_corrected.json as the input for human usefulness evaluation; keep raw final_advisor_result.json as audit trail only.

e4_required_follow_up: Normalize supporting_evidence_ids to evidence_id before persistence or Streamlit display.
Carry chunk_id as evidence metadata after binding normalization.
Make PDF source_text machine-match waiver visible in traceability/audit documentation.
thesis_wording_limitations: Do not state that all evidence source_text snippets were machine-verified against PDF text.
Use wording such as evidence pages are traceable and part of source_text matching was manually waived because of PDF extraction limitations.
Requirement-level accuracy or F1 metrics must state whether cleanup-map-normalized IDs were used.
